IT Operations & Support

Core Systems & Applications

Updates, Patching & Coordination

Coordinate with users and vendors to minimise disruption.

Promptly report security incidents or risks.

At least 3 years of IT support or operations experience.
Basic working knowledge of: 
Windows OS, Microsoft 365 applications, Desktop and laptop troubleshooting
Exposure to networking or IT support tools is an advantage.
Willingness to learn and follow structured IT processes.
Clear communication skills and a service‑oriented mindset.
Responsible, organised, and able to work well in a team environment.

We are seeking an analytical and process-driven Data Engineer to serve as the backbone of our data ecosystem, with a primary focus on maximizing the value of our Snowflake and dbt stack. In this role, you will be responsible for the reliability, efficiency, and scalability of our data transformations and storage. You will partner closely with data engineers and analysts to ensure our data platform is observable, well-governed, and resilient.
The ideal candidate possesses a deep understanding of cloud data warehousing and treats "Data as Code." You will focus on automating the data lifecycle - from ingestion to production-ready models—while ensuring that our data infrastructure remains high-performing and cost-effective.

Assist in monitoring Snowflake compute usage and managing basic user access and permission requests.
Support the data transformation lifecycle by updating models, managing documentation, and ensuring code follows our team’s standards.
Monitor daily data ingestion and transformation jobs, identifying failures and assisting in the rapid resolution of synchronization issues.
Help build and maintain automated tests to verify data accuracy and freshness before it reaches our business stakeholders.
Assist in promoting code changes from development to production environments while ensuring data integrity is maintained.
Monitor database query logs to identify slow-performing models and work with senior team members to apply SQL optimizations.
Help maintain the data catalog and lineage records to ensure all team members understand where our data comes from and how it is defined.
Provide initial root-cause analysis for pipeline errors and assist data analysts with basic technical queries regarding data availability.
Design and build new data transformations pipelines using dbt and Snowflake
Monitor resource utilization on the data platform and identify opportunities for optimization

Job Requirements:

Foundational SQL: Ability to write and understand basic to intermediate SQL queries (joins, aggregations, and subqueries).
Exposure to dbt: A basic understanding of how dbt works or experience building simple data models in a project environment.
Familiarity with Snowflake: Basic knowledge of cloud data warehouses; prior experience with Snowflake is a plus but not required.
Version Control Basics: Familiarity with Git (branching, committing, and pulling code).
Analytical Mindset: A natural curiosity for how data flows through a system and a strong attention to detail.
Communication Skills: Ability to clearly explain technical issues and work collaboratively within a team.
Eagerness to Learn: A proactive approach to picking up new tools and a desire to automate repetitive manual tasks.

required documentation, testing records, issue tracking, and control evidence.• Help identify compliance gaps, process weaknesses, and control improvement opportunities related to AI systems, workflows, and data handling practices.• Support the preparation of compliance updates, audit status reports, management summaries, and governance materials.Required Qualifications• Bachelor’s degree reporting platforms.• Experience in data wrangling, dashboard development, visualisation, and reporting.• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.• Good written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and to prepare compliance updates, summaries, and status reports.• drive accountability and follow-through across stakeholders.• Exposure to AI compliance, AI governance, AI risk management, or AI audit practices will be an advantage.• Experience in compliance Calm disposition to handle and resolve unforeseen circumstances. Good analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong interpersonal, verbal and written communication. AML Compliance SpecialistCompensation: S$3,000–S$4,500/month (dependent on experience) + 13th-month bonus + up to 3 months variable bonus.  BullionStar is Singapore’s leading precious metals dealer. We sell, buy back, store, and deliver physical gold, silver, and platinum to customers worldwide – from first-time buyers to institutional investors. We operate a full-service showroom in Singapore, a comprehensive e-commerce platform, and vault storage facilities across multiple jurisdictions.We are independently owned, founder-led, and intentionally lean. There are no layers of corporate bureaucracy here. Decisions are made quickly, everyone is close to the customer, and every person in the business has a direct impact on what we deliver. We’ve built our reputation on trust, transparency, and doing things properly – not on marketing spend.We’re looking for people who are drawn to that kind of environment – people who want ownership, not job titles, and who find energy in building something rather than maintaining something.Why This Role ExistsBullionStar operates in a regulated industry where trust is everything. Our customers entrust us with their wealth, and regulators entrust us to keep the financial system clean. AML compliance isn’t a box-ticking exercise here – it’s fundamental to our licence to operate and to the trust our customers place in us.This role exists because we need someone who understands that compliance is a competitive advantage, not a cost centre. Someone who can keep us ahead of evolving regulations, protect the business from financial crime risk, and do it all without creating unnecessary friction for legitimate customers. The right person will see patterns others miss, question things that don’t feel right, and have the confidence to act on their instincts.What You’ll OwnThink, Don’t Just Process - You’ll own the day-to-day execution of BullionStar’s AML compliance framework. That means you’re not just following procedures someone else wrote – you’re constantly evaluating whether those procedures are actually catching what they need to catch, and improving them when they’re not.Monitor Transactions with Genuine Curiosity - You’ll review alerts with real investigative instinct, not just a checklist. You’ll look beyond the obvious flags and develop a feel for what doesn’t sit right – unusual patterns, timing anomalies, behavioural shifts. When something needs reporting, you’ll prepare clear, thorough Suspicious Transaction Reports and Cash Transaction Reports and submit them to the relevant authorities promptly and accurately.Own the Full KYC and Due Diligence Process - You’ll manage the full spectrum – standard CDD, Enhanced Due Diligence for higher-risk customers, and ongoing monitoring. You won’t just collect documents; you’ll think critically about what they tell you. You’ll work directly with customers to guide them through the process, making compliance feel professional and straightforward rather than adversarial. You’ll approve or escalate cases based on your own judgement, and you’ll stand behind those decisions.Stay Ahead of Regulation - You’ll keep the company’s AML policies and procedures current – not by waiting for regulatory changes to land on your desk, but by proactively tracking developments in MAS regulations, FATF guidance, and industry best practices. When something changes, you’ll assess the impact, recommend updates, and implement them. You’ll ensure all regulatory filings are accurate and submitted on time.Make Compliance Real Across the Organisation - You’ll design and deliver training that actually sticks - engaging guidance that helps every team member understand their role in keeping BullionStar clean. You’ll work closely with operations, customer service, and leadership to ensure compliance is woven into how the business runs, not bolted on afterwards.Use Technology to Sharpen the Edge - You’ll look for ways to use AI and technology to improve our compliance capability – smarter screening tools, better pattern detection, automated reporting workflows, more efficient document processing. You’ll be hands-on with these tools, testing and integrating them into daily workflows rather than just advocating for them.Who We’re Looking ForWe care far more about who you are than what’s on your CV. The right person will have most or all of the following traits:Intelligent and sharp. You think critically, spot inconsistencies others overlook, and connect dots across seemingly unrelated data points. You don’t just process information – you interrogate it. When something doesn’t add up, you dig deeper rather than moving on.Common sense. You balance regulatory rigour with practical reality. You understand that compliance exists to protect the business and its customers, not to create obstacles for their own sake. You make sound judgement calls and stand behind them.Entrepreneurial. You treat the compliance function as if it were your own business. You think about efficiency, effectiveness, and impact – not just ticking boxes. You proactively seek out risks rather than waiting for them to find you. We’d rather see someone who has taken real initiative in a previous role than someone who has simply followed established procedures in a large institution.Curious and investigative. You have a natural instinct to question and investigate. You read beyond the surface of a transaction, a document, or a customer profile. You stay current with financial crime trends, typologies, and regulatory developments – not because you have to, but because it genuinely interests you.Strong initiative. You don’t wait to be told what to do. When you see a gap in a process, a risk that isn’t being addressed, or an opportunity to improve how things are done, you act on it. You bring proposals, not just observations.Enthusiastic. You care about getting this right. You take pride in a compliance operation that is genuinely effective, not just compliant on paper. A genuine interest in the precious metals industry and the unique AML challenges it presents is extremely desirable.AI-forward. You see technology as a way to make compliance smarter and faster. You’re willing to experiment with AI-driven screening, monitoring, and analysis tools and integrate them into daily workflows – not as a replacement for human judgement, but as a way to focus it where it matters most.Formal qualifications and years of experience in a compliance department matter far less to us than the traits above. If you have the intelligence, the instinct, and the drive, you can succeed in this role regardless of your background.What This Role Is NotThis is not a role where you mechanically process alerts and check boxes on a form. If your approach to compliance is “follow the checklist and file the report,” you’ll struggle here. We need someone who thinks, questions, and applies genuine judgement to every case.It’s not a role buried in a large compliance department where your work disappears into a process. You’ll have direct visibility, direct impact, and direct accountability. What you do matters, and everyone in the business will know it.

Compensation: Up to S$10,000/month (dependent on experience) + 13th-month + up to 3 months bonusOpen to local and international candidates.BullionStar is Singapore’s leading precious metals dealer. We sell, buy back, store, and deliver physical gold, silver, and platinum to customers worldwide – from first-time buyers to institutional investors. We operate a full-service showroom in Singapore, a comprehensive e-commerce platform, and vault storage facilities across multiple jurisdictions.We are independently owned, founder-led, and intentionally lean. There are no layers of corporate bureaucracy here. Decisions are made quickly, everyone is close to the customer, and every person in the business has a direct impact on what we deliver. We’ve built our reputation on trust, transparency, and doing things properly – not on marketing spend.We’re looking for people who are drawn to that kind of environment – people who want ownership, not job titles, and who find energy in building something rather than maintaining something.Why This Role ExistsThe payments function at BullionStar sits behind every transaction we process. When it runs well, customers barely notice. When it doesn’t, trust erodes fast. This role exists because we need someone who understands that – and takes it personally.This isn’t a complicated function at its core. But it demands sharp judgment, consistent care, and a genuine ability to read people and situations. The right person will be firm when they need to be firm, generous when circumstances warrant it, and switched on enough to surface problems before they become serious. They won’t wait to be told what to do. They’ll own it.What You'll OwnMake the Right Call, Every Time - Every payment situation needs to be assessed in full – account history, order activity, context, and the customer’s track record all matter. You’ll recognise patterns of abuse and hold the line firmly. Where a customer is facing genuine obstacles – bank restrictions, technical issues, timing problems – you’ll be generous: time extensions, cancellations, and discounts are tools, not exceptions. These are judgment calls, not policy lookups.Run the Operation with Precision - You’ll take full ownership of all incoming and outgoing customer payments – buy and sell orders, multi-currency transactions, withdrawals, and order reconciliation. You’ll build the processes and guidelines your team needs to operate with accuracy and consistency. Every transaction that flows through this function is a reflection of BullionStar’s reliability.Build and Lead the Team - You’ll hire, develop, and hold your payments team to a high standard. You’ll be in it with them – handling real cases alongside them, developing their judgment over time, and building a team that handles the majority of situations confidently and independently.Be the Early Warning System - You’ll surface what matters before it becomes a problem: volumes becoming unmanageable, a rising rate of aborted orders, customers repeatedly hitting obstacles with the same bank or financial institution, friction caused by BullionStar’s own systems or processes, and decisions about when force prepay should apply – for a specific subset of customers or more broadly. These aren’t things to quietly note. They need to be raised.Manage Banking Relationships - You’ll act as the primary point of contact for our banking partners and financial institutions. You’ll maintain those relationships, manage escalations when needed, and represent BullionStar professionally in any payment-related communication.What You'll OwnWe care far more about who you are than what’s on your CV. The right person will have most or all of the following traits:Intelligent and aware. You read situations quickly, notice details others miss, and make sound calls under pressure. You see the full picture before you act, and you act without needing to be pushed.Common sense. You don’t need a policy for every scenario. You use good judgment, own your decisions, and stay calm when things get complicated.Entrepreneurial. You’ve built something, run something, or been the person who made things happen without a roadmap. We’d rather see someone who has navigated the real challenges of a small business than someone with middle management experience from a large corporation where the rules were already set.Hands-on leader. You lead by doing, not by delegating from a distance. Your team handles more because you’re in it with them – working real cases, building their judgment, raising their standard.Aware and creative. When the process doesn’t fit the situation, you find a better way. You notice patterns others miss and you act on them.Enthusiastic. BullionStar isn’t just a job to you. This role becomes a genuine priority in your life. You care about doing it well, every day, and it shows.AI-forward. You see technology as a way to work smarter. You’re willing to experiment with AI tools and integrate them into daily workflows – not as a gimmick, but as a genuine force multiplier.Formal qualifications matter far less to us than the traits above. If you have the intelligence, the judgment, and the willingness to learn, you can succeed in this role regardless of your background.What This Role Is NotThis is not a process-following role where you apply the same rules to every situation. Payments at BullionStar require judgment, context, and decisiveness. If your instinct is to escalate everything or wait for guidance, this isn’t the right fit.It’s not a back-office position detached from the customer. You’ll be making real decisions that affect real people’s transactions, often under time pressure, and communicating directly with customers and banking partners.It’s not a role for someone who needs a corporate structure to function. You’ll have real ownership and real autonomy – and we expect you to use both.

Staying sharp is part of the job.Who We’re Looking ForMinimum academic qualification — at least one of the following:• GCE ‘A’ Level certificate with passes in at least three H2 and two H1 subjects• International Baccalaureate Diploma• Diploma from a polytechnic in Singapore•  An equivalent qualification recognised by the relevant regulatory authorityBeyond qualifications — the kind of person who fits:• Genuinely wants to help people. You’ll have a lot of it. This is not a salary — it’s startup support.• Mentorship from advisors who have walked the same path, from first clients to affluent and high net worth relationships.• Real exposure to investment management and legacy planning — working on actual client cases with proper guidance, not just theory.• In-house investment research office providing weekly market developments and investment strategies.• A collaborative team environment with opportunities to work on joint client engagements.• Training that grows with you — from foundational advisory skills through to the capabilities needed for complex, high net worth client work.• Support to get certified and stay current with industry requirements.• Regional travel as part of how we actually work.• Health and wellness benefits.About Aspire AllianceWe’re based in Downtown Tanjong Pagar and our team specialises in investment management and legacy planning advisory. Job Title: General Counsel and Head of Compliance
Job Description: We are a market leader in the precious commodities and bullion sector, with operations spanning multiple jurisdictions. The successful candidate will serve as the company's principal legal advisor while building and overseeing a robust compliance framework suited to a highly regulated, cross-border industry. lawyer who thrives at the intersection of legal counsel, compliance leadership, and commercial strategy — and who understands the unique regulatory landscape surrounding physical precious metals trading, storage, and e-commerce.Key ResponsibilitiesLegal CounselServe as the company's principal legal advisor, providing guidance on corporate, commercial, and transactional matters to the board and senior management.Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial agreements, including supply contracts, storage and custody arrangements, partnership agreements, and cross-border transaction documents.Manage and oversee external legal counsel across jurisdictions, ensuring quality, cost-effectiveness, and alignment with the company's interests.Advise on corporate structuring, governance, and intellectual property matters as the business scales.Proactively identify and manage legal risks, and lead dispute resolution efforts when they arise.Compliance & Regulatory RiskDesign, implement, and continuously improve the company's compliance framework, ensuring adherence to all applicable laws and regulations across operating jurisdictions.Monitor and interpret evolving regulatory requirements — including those related to anti-money laundering (AML), counter-terrorism financing (CTF), sanctions, know-your-customer (KYC), and the Precious Stones and Precious Metals (Prevention of Money Laundering, Terrorism Financing and Proliferation Financing) Act and related regulations in Singapore.Establish and maintain internal compliance policies, procedures, and training programmes to embed a culture of compliance across the organisation.Oversee regulatory filings, reporting obligations, and audit readiness.Conduct internal compliance reviews and risk assessments, and ensure timely remediation of any identified gaps.Act as the primary point of contact with regulators and relevant government authorities.Strategic & Commercial SupportPartner with senior leadership to integrate legal and compliance considerations into business strategy, particularly in relation to international expansion, market entry, and new product development.Conduct legal and regulatory due diligence for new markets, partnerships, and cross-border business arrangements.Advise on the structuring and negotiation of international transactions, joint ventures, and strategic investments.Anticipate regulatory and geopolitical developments that may impact the business, and provide actionable recommendations to the leadership team.Leadership & GovernanceBuild, lead, and develop the legal and compliance function, fostering a culture of accountability, integrity, and excellence.Serve as a trusted advisor to the board and executive team, balancing risk management with commercial objectives.Champion governance best practices and ensure that the company's policies evolve in line with its growth trajectory and regulatory obligations.Lead cross-functional initiatives where legal and compliance alignment is critical.Qualifications & RequirementsBachelor of Laws (LL.B) or equivalent legal qualification. A postgraduate degree (e.g., LL.M in International Trade, Financial Regulation, or Compliance) or professional compliance certifications (e.g., ICA, ACAMS) will be a strong advantage.Minimum of 7 to 10 years of post-qualification experience, with a meaningful combination of private practice and in-house counsel exposure.Strong grounding in corporate and commercial law, with demonstrable experience in contracts, regulatory compliance, and corporate governance.Direct experience with AML/CTF compliance frameworks, sanctions regimes, and regulated industries is highly preferred. Exposure to the precious metals, commodities, or financial services sector will be regarded favourably.Proven experience managing compliance programmes and engaging with regulators across multiple jurisdictions.Track record of advising on cross-border transactions, international operations, and multi-jurisdictional regulatory requirements.Exceptional leadership skills with the ability to build and manage teams, drive projects, and influence stakeholders at all levels.Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills.Highly analytical and strategic, with excellent problem-solving and decision-making capabilities.Resilient, adaptable, and capable of thriving in a fast-paced, growth-oriented organisation operating in a dynamic regulatory environment.Salary & BenefitsThe salary for this role is SGD 15,000 to SGD 28,000 per month, with the final offer dependent on the candidate's qualifications, depth of experience, and proven track record in legal and compliance leadership. Performance-based incentives and additional benefits will be discussed with shortlisted candidates.

JOB DESCRIPTIONAs an Anti-scam Specialist, you must possess excellent analytical skills along with a passion for uncovering the truth, research, discovery, and recovery to help vulnerable and unsuspecting victims. Independently assess, intercept, and manage sophisticated fraud transactions, with a particular focus on those leveraging emerging technologies, hybrid attacks combining multiple communication channels, and social engineering tactics.2. Coordinate effectively with Singapore law enforcement agencies, such as the Singapore Police Force (SPF), and understand and align with relevant regulatory changes and government initiatives in scam prevention and enforcement.5. Collect information from victims via various interaction methods and analyze/study emerging fraud trends and patterns, including the types of scams prevalent in Singapore. Maintain a comprehensive database of detected/reported frauds for timely retrieval and analysis, potentially utilizing data analytics and machine learning insights.Required Experience:1. Good analytical skills with the ability to work independently with minimum supervision in a fast-paced environment and under high pressure is essential.2. Proven experience as a Fraud Investigator in the financial sector, with a demonstrated understanding of the evolving financial crime landscape, particularly in Singapore.3. Excellent interviewing skills, including the ability to adapt questioning techniques for victims of technologically sophisticated social engineering and hybrid attacks.4. Honesty, ethics, integrity, and confidentiality are a must.5. Strong sense of ownership and pride in your performance, with a critical thinking and problem-solving mindset.6. Great team player with excellent time-management, interpersonal, and communication skills.7. Highly adaptable in an ever-evolving digital environment, with a keen interest in keeping abreast of new scam methodologies.8. Intermediate-level knowledge of MS Office, especially Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, is a must.9. Strong presentation skills, both written and oral, are required.10. Professionalism in managing internal stakeholders, building strong rapport, and coordinating across departments and with external law enforcement agencies is key.

Job Description

Job SummaryWe are hiring graduates and early‑career professionals for our NGT Program. The program is designed for individuals with strong technical foundations and basic banking knowledge who are keen to build expertise in core banking platforms. Successful candidates will receive structured training and certification on leading banking technologies, with opportunities for long‑term career development across core banking, wealth management technology, and large‑scale transformation programs.
Responsibilities
Support the implementation, enhancement, migration, and production stability of banking applications.
Process accounts, payments, transactions, and securities while ensuring accuracy and compliance.
Participate in system configuration, functional testing, and data validation.
Assist in documenting business and technical requirements to support solution design.
Support batch processing, interface management, and data reconciliation activities.
Collaborate with business, operations, compliance, and IT teams to align solutions with requirements.
Participate in incident reviews, root‑cause analysis, and service improvement initiatives.
Prepare operational reports to support monitoring and decision‑making.

Required Competencies & Certifications
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Data/Analytics, Finance, Banking, or related fields.
Basic knowledge or academic exposure to SQL for database querying.
Foundational skills in Linux/Unix environments for operations and scripting.
Proficiency in at least one programming or scripting language (Python, Shell, or Java).
Understanding of REST APIs and integration concepts.
Familiarity with Git or other version control systems.
Knowledge of batch processing principles.
Theoretical understanding of key banking concepts such as retail banking, wealth management, payments, securities, KYC/AML, and settlements.
Strong learning agility and adaptability.
Analytical mindset and structured problem‑solving approach.
High attention to detail and quality.
Clear written and verbal communication skills.
Professionalism in client‑facing settings.
Strong discipline in documentation and record keeping.

Preferred Competencies
0–3 years of relevant experience.
Motivation to pursue a career in core banking technologies.

Training & Career Development
Participate in a structured training curriculum focused on core banking systems.
Gain hands‑on project experience under the guidance of senior consultants.
Take on progressive responsibilities across functional, technical, and support areas.
Build toward roles such as Core Banking Consultant, Functional Analyst, Integration Specialist, Production Support Analyst, or Technical Specialist.

Company: GREEN GIRAFFE ASIA PACIFIC PTE. LTD.
Job Description:
Associate Compliance Officer
As Associate Compliance Officer within our global Legal & Compliance team, you will play a key role in Green Giraffe’s regulatory and governance activities. You will oversee APAC compliance (with a focus on Singapore and Australia), support client onboarding and KYC processes, and help safeguard the firm’s reputation while enabling sustainable growth.
You will work with a high degree of autonomy, acting as a trusted partner to business teams and senior stakeholders. The role combines regional responsibility with involvement in global compliance initiatives, ensuring high standards of ethical conduct, regulatory compliance and risk management.
The Team
The Legal & Compliance function supports Green Giraffe’s activities globally, working closely with teams across development equity, M&A, debt advisory and strategy in the energy transition sector.
Based in APAC, you will collaborate with colleagues across Europe and other regions, contributing to a global compliance framework while addressing local regulatory requirements. This is an opportunity to grow within an international and evolving organization.
Key Responsibilities
·       Regulatory Compliance & Risk (APAC)
·       Manage APAC compliance framework in line with MAS and ASIC requirements
·       Act as main contact for regulators and handle filings, inspections and communications
·       Monitor regulatory developments and advise business teams
·       Identify and mitigate compliance risks
KYC & Due Diligence
·       Lead KYC/CDD processes for onboarding and ongoing reviews
·       Monitor transactions for AML/sanctions risks
·       Maintain accurate, audit-ready client records
·       Improve KYC processes and documentation
Governance & Controls
·       Maintain policies and procedures aligned with global standards
·       Support internal controls, compliance registers and audits
Monitoring & Reporting
·       Conduct compliance reviews and support audits/inspections
·       Ensure timely escalation and resolution of issues
Additional Responsibilities
·       Oversee insurance tracking (TPL, D&O, PI)
·       Support compliance projects and reporting tools
·       Deliver compliance and AML training
·       Promote a strong compliance culture
Profile
·       Degree in Law, Compliance, Business or a related field
·       Experience with APAC regulations (MAS, ASIC) and KYC/AML frameworks
·       Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
·       Clear and pragmatic communicator
·       Proactive, organized and comfortable in an international environment
·       High integrity and ability to work independently
What We Offer
·       Permanent full-time role with competitive compensation and benefits
·       International exposure and cross-regional collaboration
·       Opportunities to grow with the organization
·       Dynamic, entrepreneurial and supportive environment
About Green Giraffe
Green Giraffe is an independent advisory firm with 125+ professionals worldwide, supporting the renewable energy sector through expert advice and strong market insight. We are committed to enabling the energy transition through high-quality work and robust governance.
Salary SGD90,000 to 100,00/yearly

Company Profile

Vertex Holdings is a Singapore-based venture capital investment holding company with AUM of over USD 6.5B. It is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Temasek Holdings that provides anchor funding and operational support to a global platform of venture capital partnerships and funds in key innovation hubs around the world.  
There are seven funds in the network namely: Vertex Ventures China, Vertex Ventures Israel, Vertex Ventures SEA & India, Vertex Ventures US, Vertex Ventures HC, Vertex Growth and Vertex Ventures Japan. Each Vertex network fund operates independently with separate, local investment teams in Shanghai, Beijing, Shenzhen, Tokyo, Seoul, Jakarta, Ho Chi Minh City, Bangalore, Singapore, Tel Aviv, Palo Alto and San Francisco.  
The Vertex family of funds invests in technology and healthcare opportunities in the early and growth stages, with over 300 active portfolio companies today, and a track record of growing disruptive, transformational companies into global champions.

We’re looking for an analytical, curious, and driven individual to join our team as an investment intern. If you are interested in venture capital, technology, and the startup ecosystem, we would love to speak with you.
Candidates will be actively involved in supporting the investment team at Vertex Holdings. The team evaluates investment opportunities in both funds and startups, where a key sector of interest is Deeptech. Some examples of sectors that we look at are quantum, space, robotics, AI, new materials, climate, and more.
Responsibilities

Research on different sectors and markets.
Assist in the due diligence process when analyzing investment opportunities.
Assist in preparation of investment memos for opportunities that are presented to the investment committee.

Requirements

Interest in Venture Capital and investing in startups.
Strong research capability with clear communication skills.
Knowledge of the Sustainability sector is a plus.
Team player with good interpersonal skills.

Job Title: Community Care Academy - Assistant Manager/ Manager
Job Description: We are seeking a dynamic and versatile learning & development professional to join the CommCare Academy as an Assistant Manager/Manager. This role is pivotal in curating, designing and implementing professional development programmes through strong partnerships with Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) and external stakeholders. The successful candidate will drive training needs analysis, curriculum design and programme facilitation, while identifying emerging capability gaps and opportunities to enhance overall learning impact and workforce development in SLEC and the community care sector.Key Responsibilities:External Partnerships & Stakeholder EngagementServe as the primary liaison with IHLs, professional bodies and external partners to assess and coordinate collaboration opportunities to create impactful student and institutional learning experiences.Engage external partners to co-curate professional development programmes aligned with organisational priorities and sector needs.Represent SLEC in discussions with IHLs/partners to design, plan and implement educational initiatives that promote understanding of community care and our service philosophy.Translate potential IHL learning collaborations into actionable proposals for internal stakeholders, gathering feedback and evaluating their capacity to support the partnership, including the necessary resources for implementation.2. Learning Needs Analysis & Programme DevelopmentConduct training needs analysis to identify capability gaps and professional development priorities based on internal or external learning requests.Co-develop structured training programmes with subject matter experts and relevant stakeholders, ensuring alignment with organisational goals and service priorities.Design curricula and learning journeys grounded in adult learning principles and evidence-informed pedagogical frameworks.Curate blended learning experiences (e-learning, workshops, experiential learning, workplace learning) to maximise learner engagement and impact, in alignment with our Workplace Learning initiative.3. Training Delivery & FacilitationFacilitate selected training programmes and learning interventions.Provide constructive feedback and coaching to learners to support learning transfer to the workplace.Evaluate programme effectiveness using participant feedback, learning assessments and post-training impact measures; analyse findings to inform continuous improvement.4. Innovation & Continuous ImprovementProactively identify emerging trends, skills gaps, innovation and opportunities in workforce development.Recommend enhancements to strengthen learning effectiveness, scalability and sustainability.Contribute to the overall strategy and growth of CommCare Academy.Qualifications & Requirements:ACTA/ACLP certification is preferred.At least 5 years of experience in training, learning & development, or adult education.Proven track record in conducting training needs analysis, designing curricula, applying adult learning principles and facilitating learning programmes, including hands-on delivery as a trainer.Experience working with IHLs or in the community care sector is a plus.Strong stakeholder management, proposal writing and partnership development skills.Excellent facilitation, communication and interpersonal skills.Able to manage multiple projects and priorities while maintaining high quality outcomes.Proactive, innovative and quick to identify learning gaps and opportunities to enhance impact.About St Lukes EldercareSt Lukes ElderCare (SLEC) is a Christian healthcare provider dedicated to enriching the lives of seniors in Singapore, regardless of race, language and religion.Guided by our GRACE philosophy of care, we are committed to providing compassionate and holistic care that fosters autonomy and choice. To empower elders of varying needs, from the fit to the frail, we offer a comprehensive suite of services islandwide. These include community-based programmes that promote active ageing; centre-based offerings such as day care, rehabilitation and nursing; residential (nursing home) services for long-term care; and home-based services covering medical, nursing and therapy needs.Leveraging our legacy of over 25 years, we are on an unstoppable mission to transform care challenges of Singapores ageing population. Through innovation, collaboration and education, we seek to elevate the community care sector, where elders thrive in their golden years and age with dignity, independence and joy.Incorporated in 1999, SLEC is a registered charity and an Institution of a Public Character. Since our founding with four centres, we have grown to more than 30 centres and two nursing homes, impacting over 22,000 elders islandwide.We are seeking a passionate and compassionate professional to join us. If youre ready to embrace a challenge that blends purpose with professional and personal growth, we invite you to join our Super Loving Extra Caring family to Serve, Love and Empower Communities together!

Site Name: Singapore - JurongPosted Date: Apr 22 2026Business IntroductionWe manufacture and supply reliable, high-quality medicines and vaccines to meet patients’ needs and drive our performance.
Our network of 37 medicines and vaccines manufacturing sites delivered 1.7 billion packs of medicines and 409 million vaccine doses in 2024 to help make a positive impact on the health of millions of people. Our supply chain is not just core to our operations; it’s vital to bringing our innovations to patients as quickly, efficiently and effectively as possible. Technology is transforming how we manufacture medicines and vaccines, enabling us to increase the speed, quality and scale of product supply.
We need the very best minds and capability to help us on our journey to make more complex products, harnessing the power of smart manufacturing technologies including robotics, digital solutions and artificial intelligence to deliver for patients.
Position SummaryYou will design, improve and support manufacturing processes at our Singapore site. You will work closely with operations, quality, engineering and safety teams to solve problems, deliver projects and improve performance. We value clear thinkers who act with care, collaborate well, and seek practical solutions. This role offers visible impact, broad learning, and the chance to help GSK unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together.
Responsibilities
This role will provide YOU the opportunity to lead key activities to progress YOUR career. These responsibilities include some of the following:

Identify and scope equipment, instrumentation and facility improvement projects to boost safety, quality and productivity.
Lead front-end design, vendor selection, and project execution across all phases to deliver projects on time and on budget.
Support commissioning, qualification and validation (IQ/OQ/PQ) activities and prepare test protocols and reports.
Troubleshoot process and equipment issues, use root cause analysis, and implement robust corrective actions.
Maintain compliance with current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP), health and safety, and data integrity expectations.
Create and update technical documentation, work instructions and training for operations and engineering teams.

Basic QualificationWe are seeking professionals with the following required skills and qualifications to help us achieve our goals:

Bachelor’s degree in chemical, Mechanical, Biochemical or related engineering field.
Minimum 2 years’ experience in manufacturing or process engineering in pharmaceutical, biotech, or related industry.
Hands-on experience with process troubleshooting and root cause analysis.
Experience supporting equipment commissioning, qualification or validation.
Working knowledge of cGMP and health and safety practices in a regulated manufacturing environment.
Strong communication and teamwork skills with commitment to respectful collaboration.

Work ArrangementThis position is on-site in Singapore. You will work closely with colleagues across operations, quality, engineering and safety, with occasional cross-site collaboration.
What we valueWe look for people who are curious, reliable and open to learning. We value clear, honest communication and collaborative problem solving. You will be supported to grow your skills and take on visible work that makes a real difference to patients and communities.
Ready to apply?If this role excites you, we encourage you to apply. Tell us about your experience and what you want to achieve next. We welcome applications from people with diverse experiences and career paths.
Why GSK?
Uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together.
GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. We aim to positively impact the health of 2.5 billion people by the end of the decade, as a successful, growing company where people can thrive. We get ahead of disease by preventing and treating it with innovation in specialty medicines and vaccines. We focus on four therapeutic areas: respiratory, immunology and inflammation; oncology; HIV; and infectious diseases – to impact health at scale.
People and patients around the world count on the medicines and vaccines we make, so we’re committed to creating an environment where our people can thrive and focus on what matters most. Our culture of being ambitious for patients, accountable for impact and doing the right thing is the foundation for how, together, we deliver for patients, shareholders and our people.
We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace and providing equal opportunities for all applicants. We embrace an agile working culture across our roles, so if flexibility is important to you please discuss opportunities with our hiring team. If you need any adjustments to the recruitment process to help you demonstrate your strengths and capabilities, contact APACRecruitment.Adjustments@gsk.com. Please note this email is for adjustment requests only; for other enquiries please use our standard contact channels.

Job Title: Sr Executive / Asst Manager / Sr Asst Manager, TTSH Medical Tower Project Office
Job Description: Company description:Tan Tock Seng HospitalJob description:SENIOR EXECUTIVE / ASSISTANT MANAGER / SENIOR ASSISTANT MANAGERTTSH MEDICAL TOWER PROJECT OFFICE(CLINICAL SERVICE PLANNING & HOSPITAL OPERATIONS)   The HealthCity Novena Masterplan was developed by Tan Tock Seng Hospital, as the flagship tertiary hospital of NHG Health to transform the sector into a hub for healthcare, education and research to advance patient-centric care. As part of Phase 2 of the Masterplan which was recently launched on 9 Sep 2025, the former Communicable Disease Centre 2 and Annex 2 sites will be developed into a new TTSH Medical Tower that will strengthen TTSH's healthcare capacity, advance frailty-ready and technology-enabled care, improve access to medical intervention and improve clinical care outcomes for the 1.5 million residents of Central and North Singapore.  JOB DESCRIPTION You will be part of a dynamic start-up team in TTSH in the CEO's Office to plan, design, develop and construct new patient-centric healthcare facilities for the TTSH Medical Tower under the auspices of Phase 2 of HealthCity Novena Masterplan of TTSH. The upcoming TTSH Medical Tower includes 600 inpatient beds, elective surgical facilities, specialist outpatient clinics, clinical support services (laboratory, pharmacy, radiology among others), the TTSH Emergency Department and clinical staff offices. This role provides a rare and meaningful opportunity to contribute towards shaping the healthcare landscape in Singapore and positively impacting the future health of our residents. You will be part of a team working closely with clinical and operational stakeholders to ensure the plans for their services are well drawn-up and that opportunities to improve current processes or innovate have been considered. Working with the team, you will collectively ensure that the new healthcare facilities are designed to support these plans in the long run. Your work will mainly revolve around supporting the Clinical Leads and respective Operational Leads to generate and develop their future facility's clinical service and operational plans. Job Title: Senior Executive / Assistant Manager, NCIS (Data, 2-Year Contract)
Job Description: Career in the HospitalJoin our incredible team at the National University Hospital (NUH), where we believe that a hospital is more than just a workplace. Every day, we witness the remarkable impact that our healthcare professionals can have on individuals, families, and entire communities.At NUH, we are driven by a shared purpose – to lend a helping hand to those in need, to be a source of support and healing, and to empower patients to live their best lives.We are looking for passionate and committed individuals to join us as we transform lives and create a lasting impact in the lives of our patients. The impact you make at NUH will be profound, regardless of the role you take up in the hospital.About NUHThe National University Hospital (NUH) is Singapore’s leading university hospital, with a proud heritage dating back to 1905 with the establishment of what’s now the NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine. Our strong ties with the medical school have shaped us into a unique institution, allowing us to seamlessly integrate clinical care with teaching and research. As a university hospital, we go beyond the realm of providing tertiary medical care. We are a hub of knowledge and innovation, attracting healthcare professionals who are passionate about pushing boundaries and making significant contributions to the field.About Department and Purpose of the PositionThe National University Cancer Institute, Singapore (NCIS) is a national specialist centre under the National University Health System (NUHS). It is the only public cancer centre in Singapore treating both paediatric and adult cancers in one facility.We are looking for a Senior Executive or Assistant Manager to manage corporate data and to support the Data, Informatics, Digital and Population Health at NCIS. The role involves executing data analysis projects, supporting dashboard development, and contributing to informatics initiatives under guidance from Reporting Officer, Sections Head and Head of Department.Key ResponsibilitiesPrimary Responsibilities (80%)Work with stakeholders and users to perform data analysis, identify trends and support the development of actionable insights for research projects and improvement initiatives.Ensure proper extraction, validation, and storage of data from various systems.Support the development of operational dashboards to meet the business needs and enhance quality oncology care delivery.Assist in implementing data workflows and algorithms, including data input, output and storage processes between health IT systems.Perform data analytics and/or data mining from various systems using established methodologies.Collate clinical and management information for the department and hospital as directed.Support regular reporting activities and highlight data-driven observations to Reporting Officer, Section Heads and Head of Department.Secondary Responsibilities (20%)Support IT projects and/or new system implementation activities under supervision.Assist in the maintenance of in-house application systems following established procedures.Monitor and maintain records of department's servers, workstations, and end-user equipment.Collate annual budget preparation for IT systems, equipment, and software. Assist in the coordination of acquisition and implementation processes under supervision.Support the assessment of operational needs and assist in implementing recommended IT solutions.Collaborate effectively with informatics team and contribute to achieving team KPIs.Perform other assignments as delegated by Reporting Officer, Sections Head and Head of Department.Key RequirementsA recognised degree in Mathematics, IT or Data Analytics with at least 4 years of relevant experienceGood system and data analysis and reporting skills. Knowledge in data visualisation / analytical / statistical tools (e.g., Tableau, R, Python) will be an advantage.Good organisational and ability to work collaboratively in a team environmentKnowledge of hospital operations, medical systems/applications, data analytics would be an advantage.Demonstrated ability to execute tasks efficiently and support team initiatives.Strong learning agility and willingness to develop technical and professional capabilities.Closing & ApplicationsWhen you join NUH, you become part of a dynamic and collaborative environment where expertise, knowledge, and compassion all come together. Working in healthcare offers satisfaction that extend far beyond the ordinary. The smiles of gratitude from patients and the profound sense of fulfilment that comes from helping others cannot be measured. It is a career that allows for both personal and professional growth. The challenges you encounter in this rapidly evolving industry push you to constantly expand your knowledge and skills, as well as encourage lifelong learning. Be a part of a team that gets to create a transformative healthcare experience for our patients and professionals alike.

Job Description: The upcoming Eastern General Hospital (EGH) aims to provide excellent healthcare and promote healing centred around each person.
